interface: Vault HTTP API v1 — AppRole auth method plus token renew/revoke self endpoints (stable)

headers.X-Vault-Token: sent on renew-self and revoke-self (the token being managed); NEVER sent on the unauthenticated AppRole login
headers.X-Vault-Namespace: namespace path such as team-eng/, sent on every session request when configured; omitted entirely for the root namespace
headers.Content-Type: application/json on requests that carry a body

auth_fields.accessor: safe reference to the token; may be logged

body.increment: <login lease_duration as a seconds string, e.g. 1200s>

rules: the session requests the login lease as the increment every time
the granted auth.lease_duration in the response is authoritative and re-bases the expiry at the injected clock's current time
a 403 from renew-self means the token is expired or revoked server-side: fall back to one fresh AppRole login instead of failing
revocation happens on session close/context-manager exit, at most once
a session that never obtained a token must not call Vault on close

renewal_threshold.rule: with the injected clock, remaining = obtained_at + lease_duration - clock(); the session renews (or re-logs-in when the token is not renewable) as soon as remaining <= renew_threshold * lease_duration
renewal_threshold.example: lease 1200, threshold 0.25: elapsed 899 does nothing, elapsed 900 renews

retryable_statuses.429: default return code for health of standby nodes
retryable_statuses.503: sealed, down for maintenance, or temporarily overloaded

rules: retryable responses are retried on the injected sleep schedule (retry_delays), one attempt per delay after the initial attempt
any other non-2xx (400, 403, ...) raises immediately with no sleep
exhausting the schedule raises a structured error carrying the last HTTP status and the decoded errors array

errors.structured_error: VaultServerError with .status (int) and .errors (list of str); its text includes the status but never token material

rules: client_token and secret_id must never appear in any log record, exception text, or repr
the token accessor is the documented safe identifier and may be logged
